old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-6mka.1","title":"Spec: SF assignment for orthogonal channels","description":"Define SF assignment for orthogonal channel usage.

## Location
Add to spec/02-physical-link.md

## Assignment methods

### Method 1: Hash-based (stateless)
  assigned_sf = SF7 + (hash(iid) mod 6)
- No coordination needed
- Even distribution across SFs
- Peers on different SFs cannot direct-communicate

### Method 2: Gateway-assigned (DIO option)
- ASSIGNED_SF field in DIO
- Gateway tracks node count per SF, balances load
- Node MUST use assigned SF for all TX

### Method 3: Join-based
- New node joins on SF10 (common ground)
- Gateway responds with assigned SF
- Node switches after join complete

## Cross-SF communication

When src and dst on different SFs:
1. Src TX to gateway on src_sf
2. Gateway relays to dst on dst_sf
- Adds one hop latency
- Gateway is bottleneck

## Gateway requirements
- MUST receive on all SFs (multi-SF RX or CAD scan)
- MAY use multiple radios for true parallel RX
- Single radio: cycle through SFs, ~200ms per SF = 1.2s full scan","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T18:22:28.230909Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T18:22:28.230909Z","labels":["spec","phy","scalability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-6mka.1","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-6mka","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T18:22:28.378316Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value:
{"notes":"## Backwards Compatibility

**No flag day required.**

- Old nodes: Use SF10 for all traffic (current behavior)
- New nodes: Use assigned SF from gateway
- Mixed network: SF10 is common ground for direct P2P; gateway bridges all SFs

**Requirements:**
- SF10 MUST remain the default SF for nodes without assignment
- Old nodes stay on SF10, can communicate directly with each other
- New nodes on non-SF10 MUST route through gateway to reach SF10 nodes
- Gateway MUST receive on all SFs (multi-SF RX or CAD scan)
- Direct P2P fallback: new nodes MAY use SF10 for direct P2P if peer is on SF10

**Communication matrix:**
| Src | Dst | Path |
|-----|-----|------|
| Old (SF10) | Old (SF10) | Direct |
| Old (SF10) | New (SF7) | Via gateway |
| New (SF7) | Old (SF10) | Via gateway OR fallback to SF10 direct |
| New (SF7) | New (SF7) | Direct |
| New (SF7) | New (SF9) | Via gateway |

**Degradation:**
- Cross-SF traffic adds gateway hop
- Old nodes see no change
- Benefit scales with gateway capacity and new node fraction"}
